scenario.label: L8.4 Circular pinhole aperture edge-diffraction external FDTD diagnostic

source.label: 500 nm coherent plane wave for aperture edge diagnostic

elements.label: Circular pinhole aperture
elements.validation: external FDTD fixture compared to scalar limiting-case reference and convergence diagnostics

boundary: Limited aperture/blocker edge-diffraction diagnostics only: long slit, circular pinhole, rectangular aperture, and opaque blocker.
External FDTD export/import evidence only; the browser app does not execute FDTD or arbitrary 3D Maxwell.
Scalar sinc/Airy references are limiting-case checks, not exact finite-screen FDTD truth.
Finite screen size, screen thickness, staircasing, PML placement, monitor placement, and near-field/far-field interpretation require convergence review.
Ideal reflective screens are diagnostics only, not production metal aperture models.
No arbitrary CAD aperture, conformal aperture-edge coating, curved/freeform aperture surface, FEM/BEM/RCWA execution, sensor-stack EM, digital twin, or manufacturing certification is claimed.

manifest.label: L8.1 external FDTD export for L8.4 Circular pinhole aperture edge-diffraction external FDTD diagnostic

boundaries.note: PML boundary scaffold for optional external Meep execution.

supported.label: 500 nm coherent plane wave for aperture edge diagnostic
supported.evidence: plane-wave source exported as Meep source scaffold

supported.label: Circular pinhole aperture
supported.evidence: finite surface geometry export with L8.3 validation warnings

supported.label: Air observation reference
supported.evidence: transparent dielectric slab/block export with Fresnel/TMM comparison

warnings.message: Circular pinhole aperture has flux monitors within two wavelengths of the scatterer in the generated helper scene; move monitors farther away for production convergence runs.
Circular pinhole aperture has aperture edges; treat downstream fields as diagnostic until resolution/PML convergence is shown.
Circular pinhole aperture finite FDTD screen is compared only to scalar limiting-case diffraction references; finite thickness and finite screen size can shift residuals.

limitations: External FDTD export/import only; the browser app does not execute FDTD.
Generated Meep scripts are deterministic helper artifacts for optional local execution.
Imported field/flux maps are external-run evidence with receipts, not in-browser Maxwell solves.
L8.3 finite surface geometry is limited to placed transparent blocks, absorbing blocks, ideal reflector diagnostics, aperture/blocker masks, and tilted/wedge diagnostics with convergence warnings.

L8.4 aperture/blocker edge-diffraction validation is limited to long-slit, circular-pinhole, rectangular-aperture, and opaque-blocker diagnostic scenes with scalar limiting-case references and convergence warnings.

L8.5 multi-element optical bench export/import composes supported finite geometry into deterministic external FDTD helper scenes with monitor definitions and receipts; it is not an in-browser FDTD solve.

No arbitrary 3D CAD geometry, curved material lens solve, finite-thickness metal aperture Maxwell solve, FEM/BEM/RCWA execution, production solver validation, sensor-stack EM, digital twin, or manufacturing certification is claimed.

script.python:
# Generated by EMMicro L8.1 External FDTD / Field Maps.
# Optional external helper only; the browser app does not execute FDTD.
# source_scenario_hash: c4f75c6515cf0057
# manifest_hash: 4988876e01a19248

import json
import meep as mp

cell_size = mp.Vector3(16, 16, 60)
resolution = 16
pml_layers = [mp.PML(0.75)]

materials = {
    "air": mp.Medium(index=1),  # k=0 hash=2653810c8f22d751
    "target-dielectric": mp.Medium(index=1),  # k=0 hash=9a86ca1878c587aa
    "element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker": mp.Medium(index=1.1),  # k=0.05 hash=69be4ab53b4d23f8
}

geometry = [
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 58), size=mp.Vector3(16, 16, 100), material=materials["target-dielectric"]),  # transparent-slab
    # finite-aperture-blocker: circular-pinhole segmented screen helper; Meep run should use convergence/subpixel review before interpretation
    # aperture_radius_um=2 screen_radius_um=6
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(3.69551813005, 1.53073372946, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(0.923879532511, 0.382683432365, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(-0.382683432365, 0.923879532511, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(1.53073372946, 3.69551813005, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(0.382683432365, 0.923879532511, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(-0.923879532511, 0.382683432365, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(-1.53073372946, 3.69551813005, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(-0.382683432365, 0.923879532511, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(-0.923879532511, -0.382683432365, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(-3.69551813005, 1.53073372946, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(-0.923879532511, 0.382683432365, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(-0.382683432365, -0.923879532511, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(-3.69551813005, -1.53073372946, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(-0.923879532511, -0.382683432365, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(0.382683432365, -0.923879532511, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(-1.53073372946, -3.69551813005, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(-0.382683432365, -0.923879532511, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(0.923879532511, -0.382683432365, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(1.53073372946, -3.69551813005, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(0.382683432365, -0.923879532511, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(0.923879532511, 0.382683432365, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
    mp.Block(center=mp.Vector3(3.69551813005, -1.53073372946, 25), size=mp.Vector3(4, 3.14159265359, 0.8), material=materials["element-l84-circular-pinhole-blocker"], e1=mp.Vector3(0.923879532511, -0.382683432365, 0), e2=mp.Vector3(0.382683432365, 0.923879532511, 0), e3=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 1)),
]

sources = [
    mp.Source(mp.ContinuousSource(wavelength=0.5), component=mp.Ez, center=mp.Vector3(0, 0, 4), size=mp.Vector3(16, 16, 0)),
]

sim = mp.Simulation(cell_size=cell_size, boundary_layers=pml_layers, geometry=geometry, sources=sources, resolution=resolution)

# Monitor definitions. External postprocess writes flux_summary.json and field_slice_xz.csv.
# monitor incident-flux: flux-plane center=(0, 0, 104) size=(16, 16, 0) normal=+z
# monitor reflected-flux: flux-plane center=(0, 0, 54) size=(16, 16, 0) normal=-z
# monitor transmitted-flux: flux-plane center=(0, 0, 55) size=(16, 16, 0) normal=+z
# monitor field-slice-xz: field-slice center=(0, 0, 30) size=(16, 0, 60) normal=+y
# monitor l84-circular-pinhole-front-flux: flux-plane center=(0, 0, 23.8) size=(12, 12, 0) normal=+z
# monitor l84-circular-pinhole-back-flux: flux-plane center=(0, 0, 26.2) size=(12, 12, 0) normal=+z

# sim.run(until=200)
# sim.output_efield_z()
# Use tools/fdtd/postprocess_meep_output.py to produce importable JSON/CSV summaries.

reference.invariant: Circular aperture scalar limiting case: first dark ring is near 1.22 lambda z / D.

warnings.message: Circular pinhole aperture has flux monitors within two wavelengths of the scatterer in the generated helper scene; move monitors farther away for production convergence runs.
Circular pinhole aperture has aperture edges; treat downstream fields as diagnostic until resolution/PML convergence is shown.
Circular pinhole aperture finite FDTD screen is compared only to scalar limiting-case diffraction references; finite thickness and finite screen size can shift residuals.
Scalar diffraction reference is a limiting-case check for diagnostic comparison, not an exact finite FDTD screen solution.
Near-field slices are not the same as far-field diffraction patterns; use far-field or downstream observation evidence with convergence.
Current aperture/blocker diagnostics require convergence sweep evidence before physical interpretation.
Flux monitor is within two wavelengths of aperture/blocker edges.
Aperture edges are staircasing-sensitive at this grid density; increase resolution and compare residual trends.

limitations: Limited aperture/blocker edge-diffraction diagnostics only: long slit, circular pinhole, rectangular aperture, and opaque blocker.
External FDTD export/import evidence only; the browser app does not execute FDTD or arbitrary 3D Maxwell.
Scalar sinc/Airy references are limiting-case checks, not exact finite-screen FDTD truth.
Finite screen size, screen thickness, staircasing, PML placement, monitor placement, and near-field/far-field interpretation require convergence review.
Ideal reflective screens are diagnostics only, not production metal aperture models.
No arbitrary CAD aperture, conformal aperture-edge coating, curved/freeform aperture surface, FEM/BEM/RCWA execution, sensor-stack EM, digital twin, or manufacturing certification is claimed.
